_comment: Optional user personalization for the Soniox engines. Copy this file to 'personal_settings.json' and customize the vocabulary block. If 'personal_settings.json' is absent, SONIOX_CONTEXT stays None and no personalization is sent. The 'terms' list is used by every Soniox engine — Soniox Live and both the v2 sync and async paths of the Soniox upload slot; the 'general' domain/topic block is used only by the newer engines (Soniox Live and the async upload path), as the legacy v2 sync path has no equivalent for it. The Groq APIs ignore this file entirely.

general.value: Your main topics here (e.g. software development, academic writing, specific field)

terms: Technical term the model often misrecognizes
Foreign word commonly used in your dictation

push_to_talk._comment: Opt-in push-to-talk (Issue #66): tap the trigger key, release, then press-and-HOLD it; recording runs while held, releasing inserts the transcript. DEFAULT OFF -- set enabled to true to use it (the gesture then reads every trigger press). On German QWERTZ, AltGr (Ctrl+Alt) is filtered out, so typing @ \ { } [ ] | euro ~ never triggers it. trigger: lctrl (default, bottom-left) | rctrl (AltGr-safe) | lalt. insert: clipboard (default, paste like Ctrl+Alt+D) | type (fallback, simulated typing like Ctrl+Alt+A, for apps that block paste) | send | no_insert. The three thresholds are seconds (positive numbers): tap_window_s = first release to second press, min_hold_s = how long the second press is held before recording starts, release_tail_s = extra time kept after release so word-ends are not clipped.

soniox_endpointing._comment: OPTIONAL, OFF BY DEFAULT (Issue #121). Fine-tunes Soniox v5 real-time endpoint detection for the Soniox Live path only (the async upload path has no endpointing). With no keys here, nothing is sent and Soniox uses its own defaults -- behavior is unchanged. To tune, add any of: 'endpoint_sensitivity' (number -1.0..1.0, default 0.0; lower waits longer before ending a sentence -- Soniox documents -0.3 for dictation and slow speakers), 'endpoint_latency_adjustment_level' (integer 0..3, default 0; higher ends sentences sooner -- leave at 0 when using a negative sensitivity), 'max_endpoint_delay_ms' (number 500..3000, default 2000; hard cap on the wait after speech stops). Out-of-range or wrong-typed values are ignored with a warning and never sent. Concrete tuning is still being decided (follow-up issue).
